selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = llm
topic = How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
persona = null

response_raw: {"mode": "llm", "topic": "How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.", "opinion": "As an AI specializing in evidence-based information, it's crucial to approach conversations about healthcare and COVID-2023 guidelines. When addressing these topics, prioritize sourcing from reputable health organizations like the WHO or CDC. Encourage users to consult direct resources for personalized advice. Clarify that while data-driven insights are provided, individual circumstances may necessitate consulting a healthcare professional. Maintain an educational stance over advocacy. Promote critical evaluation of sources and highlight updates in guidelines as new evidence emerges. Avoid sharing unverified claims to maintain trust and accuracy.","reasoning_summary": "To ensure accurate information dissemination on COVID-2023 vaccines and healthcare discussions: 1) Cite authoritative bodies (WHO, CDC). 2) Advise consulting professionals for personal guidance. 3) Uphold educational role over advocacy. 4) Encourage source evaluation by users. 5) Remain updated with evolving guidelines. Emphasize safety and verification to build trust.","tone": "Objective, Informative","citations":["World Health Organization (WHO)", "Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)"],"safety_notes":"<None>","used_persona_fields":["Educational Role", "Evidence-Based"]}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion: As an AI specializing in evidence-based information, it's crucial to approach conversations about healthcare and COVID-2023 guidelines. When addressing these topics, prioritize sourcing from reputable health organizations like the WHO or CDC. Encourage users to consult direct resources for personalized advice. Clarify that while data-driven insights are provided, individual circumstances may necessitate consulting a healthcare professional. Maintain an educational stance over advocacy. Promote critical evaluation of sources and highlight updates in guidelines as new evidence emerges. Avoid sharing unverified claims to maintain trust and accuracy.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To ensure accurate information dissemination on COVID-2023 vaccines and healthcare discussions: 1) Cite authoritative bodies (WHO, CDC). 2) Advise consulting professionals for personal guidance. 3) Uphold educational role over advocacy. 4) Encourage source evaluation by users. 5) Remain updated with evolving guidelines. Emphasize safety and verification to build trust.
citations: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)
